questions.question_html: What is the disadvantage in comparing line segments by mere observation?
questions.solution_html: Sometimes the difference in lengths between two line segments is not obvious. So, we are not always sure about our usual judgement.

questions.question_html: Why is it better to use a divider than a ruler, while measuring the length of a line segment?
questions.solution_html: These may be errors due to the thickness of the ruler and angular viewing by using a ruler. These errors are eradicated by using a divider. So, it is better to use a divider, with a ruler, while measuring the length of a line segment.

questions.question_html:
Draw any line segment, say {tex}\overline{A B}{/tex} . Take any point C lying in between A and B. Measure the lengths of AB, BC and AC. Is AB = AC + CB?
[Note: If A, B, C are any three points on a line such that AC + CB = AB, then we can be sure that C lies between A and B.]
questions.solution_html:
Length of AB = 7cm
Length of BC = 3cm
Length of AC = 4cm
AC + CB = 4cm + 3cm = 7cm
and AB = 7cm
So, AB = AC + CB.

questions.question_html: If A, B, C are three points on a line such that AB = 5cm, BC = 3cm and AC = 8cm, which one of them lies between the other two?
questions.solution_html: The point B lies between the point A and Point C.

questions.question_html: Verify whether D is the mid-point of {tex}\overline{A G}{/tex} .
questions.solution_html: Yes, D is the mid-point of {tex}\overline{A G}{/tex} (because, AD = DG = 3 units.)

questions.question_html: If B is the mid point of {tex}\overline{A C}{/tex}  and C is the point of {tex}\overline{\mathrm{BD}}{/tex} . where A, B, C, D lie on a straight line, say why AB = CD?
questions.solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} B is the mid-point of {tex}\overline{A C}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = BC ...(1)
 {tex}\because{/tex} C is the mid-point of {tex}\overline{B D}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} BC = CD ...(2)
In view of (1) and (2), we get
AB = CD.

questions.question_html: Draw five triangles and measure their sides. Check in each case, if the sum of the lengths of any two sides is always less than the third side.
questions.solution_html:
This question should be practiced by the students by themselves. Randomly draw some triangles and measure their lengths.
For example, we have drawn a few for your reference.

Here, AB = 1 cm, BC = 1 m, AC = 1 cm
Clearly, Sum of two sides > third side

Here, AB = 4 cm, BC = 3 cm and AC = 5 cm
Sum of two sides > third side

Here, AB = 3 cm, BC = 3 cm and AC = 5 cm
Sum of two sides > third side

Here, AB = 7 cm, BC = 7 cm and AC = 6 cm
Sum of two sides > third side

Here, AB = 8 cm, BC = 9 cm and AC = 10 cm
Sum of two sides > third side

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 3 to 9
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 3 to 9 it complete half of a single revolution which is 180° out of 360°.
So, the Fraction = {tex}\frac{180}{360}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
As we know 180° is the half of the 360°, so it covers {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}.

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 4 to 7
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 4 to 7 it makes a right angle which is of 90°.
So, the required Fraction = {tex}\frac{90}{360}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 7 to 10
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 7 to 10 it makes a right angle which is of 90°.
So, the fraction = {tex}\frac{90}{360}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 12 to 9
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 12 to 9 it basically covers three right angles which is of = 90 + 90 + 90 = 270°.
Therefore, required Fraction = {tex}\frac{270}{360}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 1 to 10
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 1 to 10 it basically covers three right angles which is of 270°.
So, required Fraction = {tex}\frac{270}{360}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: What fraction of a clockwise revolution does the hour hand of a clock turn through, when it goes from 6 to 3
questions.solution_html:
By looking at the clock we can see when the hour hand goes from 6 to 3 it basically covers three right angles which is of 270°.
Therefore, required Fraction = {tex}\frac{270}{360}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_html: Where will the hand of a clock stop if it starts at 12 and makes {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of a revolution, clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
In one complete revolution the hand of clock covers the 360°.
When the hand of the clock starts from 12 and makes half of the revolution clockwise, so it will stop at 6 because half of the revolution is 180°, which it covers upto 6.

questions.question_html: Where will the hand of a clock stop if it starts at 2 and makes {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of a revolution, clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
In one complete revolution the hand of clock covers the 360°.
When the hand of the clock starts from 2 and makes half of the one single revolution clockwise which is of 180°, it will stop at 8.

questions.question_html: Where will the hand of a clock stop if it starts at 5 and makes {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} of a revolution, clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
In one complete revolution the hand of clock covers the 360°.
When hand of the clock starts from 5 and makes one fourth of a revolution clockwise, which is a right angle (90°), It will stop at 8.

questions.question_html: Where will the hand of a clock stop if it starts at 5 and makes {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} of a revolution, clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
In one complete revolution the hand of clock covers the 360°. 
When the hand of a clock starts from 5 and makes {tex}\frac34th{/tex} of the revolution clockwise which is of 120°, so it will stop at 2.

questions.question_html: Which direction will you face if you start facing east and make {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of a revolution clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
When one revolve one complete round in either clockwise or anti-clockwise direction he complete an angle of 360° and the two adjacent directions will be at 90°.
Therefore, If one starts from East and makes half of the complete revolution clockwise, he will be facing the west direction.

questions.question_html:
Which direction will you face if you start facing south and make one full revolution?
(Should we specify clockwise or anti-clockwise? Why not?)
questions.solution_html:
When we revolve one complete round in either clockwise or anti-clockwise direction we complete an angle of 360° and the two adjacent directions will be at 90°.
If we start from South and make a complete revolution clockwise or anti-clockwise, we will be facing the South direction again as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: Which direction will you face if you start facing east and make {tex}1\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of a revolution clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
When we revolve one complete round in either clockwise or anti-clockwise direction we complete an angle of 360° and the two adjacent directions will be at 90°.
Therefore, If we start from East and make one and half of the complete revolution clockwise, we will be facing the west direction as shown below.

questions.question_html: Which direction will you face if you start facing west and make {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} of a revolution anti-clockwise?
questions.solution_html:
When we revolve one complete round in either clockwise or anti-clockwise direction we complete an angle of 360° and the two adjacent directions will be at 90°.
If we start from West and make three fourth of the complete revolution anti-clockwise, we will be facing the north direction as shown in figure.

questions.question_html: What part of a revolution have you turned through if you stand facing east and turn clockwise to face north?
questions.solution_html:
As we know that if we complete one revolution whether clockwise or anti-clockwise we will be making an angle of 360°.
If we start from East and turn clockwise to face north then we will be completing the three fourth of the revolution which is of 270° as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: What part of a revolution have you turned through if you stand facing south and turn clockwise to face east?
questions.solution_html:
As we know that if we complete one revolution whether clockwise or anti-clockwise we will be making an angle of 360°.
If we start from South and turn clockwise to face East then we will be completing the three fourth of the revolution which is of 270° as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: What part of a revolution have you turned through if you stand facing west and turn clockwise to face east?
questions.solution_html:
As we know that if we complete one revolution whether clockwise or anti-clockwise we will be making an angle of 360°.
If we start from West and turn clockwise to face east then we will be completing the half of the revolution which is of 180° as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angle turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 3 to 6.
questions.solution_html:
A clock hand makes an angle of 360° in one complete round which also makes of 4 right angles.
When a clock hand moves from 3 to 6 it covers only one right angle as it covers only one fourth of one complete revolution.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angles turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 2 to 8
questions.solution_html: We know that a clock hand makes an angle of 360° in on complete round which is also made of 4 right angles. When a clock hand goes from 2 to 8, it makes 2 right angles as it covers half of the complete revolution which is 180°.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angles turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 5 to 11
questions.solution_html: We know that a clock hand makes an angle of 360° in on complete round which is also made of 4 right angles. When a clock hand goes from 5 to 11, it makes 2 right angles as it covers half of one complete revolution which is 180°.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angle turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 10 to 1
questions.solution_html: We know that clock hand makes an angle of 360° in on complete round which is also made of 4 right angles. When a clock hand goes from 10 to 1 it makes only 1 right angle as it covers only one-fourth of the complete revolution.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angle turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 12 to 9.
questions.solution_html: We know that clock hand makes an angle of 360° in one complete round which is also made of 4 right angles. When a clock hand moves from 12 to 9, it makes 3 right angles as it covers three fourth of the complete revolution which is of 270°.

questions.question_html: Find the number of right angles turned through by the hour hand of a clock when it goes from 12 to 6.
questions.solution_html: We know that clock hand makes an angle of 360° in on complete round which is also made of 4 right angles. When a clock hand goes from 12 to 6, it makes 2 right angles as it covers half of the complete revolution which is of 180°.

questions.question_html: How many right angle do you make if you start facing south and turn clockwise to west?
questions.solution_html:
One complete revolution is of 360° or we can say 4 right angles.
If you start from South and turn clockwise to west then you are making 1 right angle as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: How many right angle do you make if you start facing north and turn anti-clockwise to east?
questions.solution_html:
One complete revolution is of 360° or we can say 4 right angles.
If you start from North and turn anti-clockwise to east then you are making 3 right angles as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: How many right angle do you make if you start facing west and turn to west?
questions.solution_html:
One complete revolution is of 360° or we can say 4 right angles.
If you start from west and turn to west again then you are completing one revolution which is of 4 right angles,
as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: How many right angles do you make if you start facing south and turn to north?
questions.solution_html:
One complete revolution is of 360° or we can say 4 right angles.
If you start from South and turn clockwise to north then you are making 2 right angles as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: Where will the hour hand of a clock stop if it starts from 6 and turns through 1 right angle?
questions.solution_html: As we know that one complete revolution is of 360° which consists of 4 right angles. By looking at the clock we can say that If the hour hand of the clock start from 6 and make 1 right angle then it will stop at 9.

questions.question_html: Where will the hour hand of a clock stop if it start from 8 and turns through 2 right angles?
questions.solution_html:
As we know one complete revolution is of 360° which consists of 4 right angles.
By looking at the clock we can say that If the hour hand of the clock start from 8 and make 2 right angles then it will stop at 2.

questions.question_html: Where will the hour hand of a clock stop if it start from 10 and turns through 3 right angles?
questions.solution_html:
As we know one complete revolution is of 360° which consists of 4 right angles.
By looking at the clock we can say that if the hour hand of the clock start from 10 and make 3 right angles then it will stop at 7.

questions.question_html: Where will the hour hand of a clock stop if it starts from 7 and turns through 2 straight angles?
questions.solution_html: As we know one complete revolution is 360° which is consists of 4 right angles. By looking at the clock we can say that if the hour hand of the clock starts from 7 and make 2 straight angles then it will surely stop at 7.

questions.question_html:
Match the following:

(a) Straight angle (i) Less than one-fourth a revolution
(b) Right angle (ii) More than half a revolution
(c) Acute angle (iii) Half of a revolution
(d) Obtuse angle (iv) One-fourth a revolution
(v)Reflex angle (v)Between {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}  and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}  of a revolution
  (vi)One complete revolution
Classify the angle as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex:
questions.solution_html: It is a Reflex angle as its measure is more than 180o but less 360o.

questions.question_html: Classify the angle as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex:
questions.solution_html: Since the measure is less than 90o, it is an acute angle.

questions.question_html: Classify the angle as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex :
questions.solution_html: It is an obtuse angle because its measure lies between 90o  and 180o.

questions.question_html: Classify the angle as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex:
questions.solution_html: It is an Acute angle as its measure is less than 90o.

questions.question_html: Classify the angle given below as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex:
questions.solution_html: It is a Straight angle as its measure is 180o.

questions.question_html: Classify the angle as right, straight, acute, obtuse or reflex:
questions.solution_html: Since its measure is 90o . It is a Right angle.

questions.question_html: What is the measure of a right angle?
questions.solution_html: The measure of a right angle is always of 90°

questions.question_html: What is the measure of a straight angle?
questions.solution_html: A straight angle always measures 180°

questions.question_html: The measure of an acute angle < 90°.
The measure of an obtuse angle < 90°.
The measure of a reflex angle > 180°.
The measure of one complete revolution = 360°.
If m{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 53o and m{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 35o, then m{tex}\angle{/tex}A > m{tex}\angle{/tex}B.
Write down the measures of some acute angles. Give at least two examples.
questions.solution_html: Acute angle is the angle whose measure is less than 90° so the examples are; 30°, 45°, 60° and 70°.

questions.question_html: Write down the measures of some obtuse angles. Give at least two examples.
questions.solution_html: Obtuse angle is the angle which is greater than 90° but less than 180°. The examples are: 110°, 120°, 135° and 170°.

questions.question_html: Measure the angle given below using the Protractor and write down the measure.
questions.solution_html: On measuring the angle we get its value as 45o

questions.question_html: Measure the angle given below using the protractor and write down the measure.
questions.solution_html: Using protractor the measure comes out to be 120o

questions.solution_html: On measuring with a protractor the measures of the required angles are 60o, 130o and 90o

questions.question_html: Measure the angle given below using the Protractor and write down the measure.

questions.question_html:
Which angle has a large measure? First estimate and then measure.

Measure of Angle A, Measure of Angle B.
questions.solution_html:
Measure of Angle A = 40°.
Measure of Angle B = 65°.
The angle B has a larger measure.

questions.question_html: From these two angles which has larger measure? Estimate and then confirm by the measuring them.
questions.solution_html:
Measure of first angle = 45°
Measure of second angle = 60°.
The second angle has larger measure.

When the sum of the measures of two angles is that of a straight angle and if one of them is acute then the other should be ________.

questions.solution_html: By measuring the angle with the help of protractor we see that the angle is 65 degree.

questions.question_html: Find the measure of the angle shown in figure. (First estimate with your eyes and then find the actual measure with a protractor).
questions.solution_html: By measuring the angle with the help of protractor we get the angle as 135o in the figure.

questions.solution_html: By measuring the figure with the help of protractor, we get that the measure of the angle as 40o.

questions.question_html: Find the angle measure between the hands of the clock in a figure:
questions.solution_html: Required angle = {tex}\frac{360}{12}{/tex} = 30°

questions.question_html: Find the angle measure between the hands of the clock in a figure:
questions.solution_html: Required angle is 180° as it is forming a straight line which is always of 180°.

questions.question_html: Find the angle measure between the hands of the clock in a figure:

questions.question_html:
Which of the following are models for perpendicular lines :

  1. The adjacent edges of a table top.
  2. The lines of a railway track.
  3. The line segments forming the letter "L".
  4. The letter V.
questions.solution_html: (a) and (c) are models for perpendicular lines.

questions.question_html: Let {tex}\overline{\mathrm{PQ}}{/tex}  be the perpendicular to the line segment {tex}\overline{X Y}{/tex} . Let {tex}\vec{P Q}{/tex} and {tex}\overline{X Y}{/tex}  intersect in the point A. What is the measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}PAY?
questions.solution_html: The measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}PAY is 90o.

questions.question_html: There are two set-squares in your box. What are the measure of the angles that are formed at their corners? Do they have any angle measure that is common?
questions.solution_html: One is a 30° - 60° - 90° set square; the other is a 45° - 45° - 90° set square. The angle of measure 90° (i.e. a right angle) is common between them.

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m is CE = EG?
questions.solution_html: Yes, CE = EG as both have same distance of 2 units from the point of intersection.

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m does PE bisect CG?
questions.solution_html: Yes, PE bisect CG as E is the mid-point of CG and PE divides the line segment into two equal parts which is CE = EG

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m identify any two line segments for which PE is the perpendicular bisector.
questions.solution_html: The two line segments can be taken as BH and CG.

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m, Is AC > FG?
questions.solution_html:
Yes, AC > FG is True
As length of AC = 2 units
Length of FG = 1 units

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m Is CD = GH?
questions.solution_html:
Yes, CD = GH
Since both are of the same length viz. 1 unit

questions.question_html: Study the diagram. The line l is perpendicular to line m, Is BC < EH?
questions.solution_html:
Yes, BC < EH
Because, length of BC = 1 units
Length of EH = 3 units

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: Triangle with lengths of sides 7 cm, 8 cm and 9 cm
questions.solution_html: It is a scalene triangle as it has all unequal sides.

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC with AB = 8.7 cm, AC = 7 cm and BC = 6 cm
questions.solution_html: {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is a scalene triangle as it has three unequal sides.

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR such that PQ = QR = PR = 5 cm.
questions.solution_html: {tex}\triangle{/tex} PQR is equilateral triangle as all sides of triangle are equal and according to the property of equilateral triangle has all equal sides.

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF with {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 90°
questions.solution_html: {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF is a Right-angled triangle as it has {tex}\angle{/tex} D = 90o

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: {tex}\triangle{/tex}XYZ with {tex}\angle{/tex}Y = 90° and XY = YZ.
questions.solution_html: {tex}\triangle{/tex}XYZ Right-angled isosceles angle as it has one right angle of 90o and two equal sides.

questions.question_html: Name the type of triangle: {tex}\triangle{/tex}LMN with {tex}\angle{/tex}L = 30°, {tex}\angle{/tex}M = 70° and {tex}\angle{/tex}N = 80°.
questions.solution_html: {tex}\triangle{/tex} LMN is an acute angle as it has all angles less than 90o and according to property of acute angles it is a triangle with all three angles as acute (less than 90o).

questions.question_html:
Match the following: 

Measures of Triangle Type of Triangle
(a) 3 sides of equal length (i) Scalene
(b) 2 sides of equal length (ii) Isosceles right angled 
(c) All sides are of different length (iii) Obtuse angled
(d) 3 acute angles (iv) Right angled
(e) 1 right angle (v) Equilateral
(f) 1 obtuse angle (vi) Acute angled
(g)1 right angle with two sides of equal length (vii) Isosceles
questions.solution_html:
We can match the above as follows:
(a) - (v), (b) - (vii), (c) - (i), (d) - (vi), (e) - (iv), (f) - (iii), (g) - (ii)

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html: It is an Obtuse-angled and isosceles triangle. Since we can see one angle is greater than 90o and it has two equal sides which are the property of the isosceles triangle.

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html: It is a Right-angled and isosceles triangle. As it has one angle of 90o and two equal sides which is the property of an isosceles triangle.

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html: It is an Acute-angled and equilateral triangle. As in the figure we can see all angles are less than 90o and it has all sides equal and this is the property of an equilateral triangle.

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html:
It is an Obtuse-angled and scalene triangle.
As we can see one angle is greater than 90o and three unequal sides and according to the property of triangles only scalene triangle has this property.

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html: It is an Acute-angled and isosceles triangle. As in this figure, we can see all angles are less than 90o and it has two equal sides which is the property isosceles triangle.

questions.question_html: Name triangle in two different ways: (you may judge the nature of the angle by observation)
questions.solution_html: It is a Right-angled scalene triangle. Since the triangle has one right angle and three unequal sides and these are the property of right-angled and scalene triangle.

questions.question_html:
Try to construct triangle using 4 match sticks. Some are shown here.

Name the type of triangle in given case. If you cannot make a triangle, think of reasons for it.
questions.solution_html: By using 4 matchsticks it is not possible to make a triangle as in a triangle, sum of the two sides is greater than the length of the remaining side.

questions.question_html:
Try to construct triangle using 3 match sticks. Some are shown here.

Name the type of triangle in given case. If you cannot make a triangle, think of reasons for it.
questions.solution_html: Clearly, we can make a triangle by using 3 matchsticks. According to the property of a triangle, the sum of two sides is greater than the length of the remaining side. It is an equilateral triangle as it has all equal sides.

questions.question_html:
Try to construct triangle using 5 match sticks. Some are shown here.

Name the type of triangle in given case. If you cannot make a triangle, think of reasons for it.
questions.solution_html: Yes, we can form a triangle by using 5 matchsticks as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html:
Try to construct triangle using 6 match sticks. Some are shown here.

Name the type of triangle in given case. If you cannot make a triangle, think of reasons for it.
questions.solution_html: With the help of 6 matchsticks we can form a triangle as shown in figure below.

questions.question_html: The opposite sides of a trapezium are parallel.

options.explanation: False; as in general only one pair of opposite sides is parallel.

questions.question_html: All the sides of a parallelogram are of equal length.
The diagonals of a square are perpendicular to one another.

options.explanation:
True.
Diagonals of a square are perpendicular to one another.

questions.question_html: Each angle of a rectangle is a right angle.

options.explanation:
True.
By definition, all the angles of a rectangle are 90 degree.

questions.question_html: The opposite sides of a rectangle are equal in length.

options.explanation:
True
It is a property of a rectangle that its opposite sides are equal.

questions.question_html: All the sides of a rhombus are of equal length.

options.explanation:
True
A rhombus is a quadrilateral in which all sides are of equal length.

questions.question_html: Give reason for a square can be thought of as a special rectangle.
questions.solution_html: Yes, a square is a special rectangle, as a rectangle has its all angle of 90o and opposite sides are equals to each other. In the case of a square, all the angles are also 90o and it has all the sides equals to each other. So, it is a special rectangle.

questions.question_html: Give reason that a rectangle can be thought of as a special parallelogram.
questions.solution_html: A rectangle has all its angles of 90o and opposite sides are equals and parallel to each other. A parallelogram also has opposite sides equal and parallel to each other. So we can say that a parallelogram with all of its angles as right angles becomes a rectangle and this rectangle can be thought of as a special parallelogram.

questions.question_html: Give reason for squares, rectangles, parallelograms are all quadrilaterals.
questions.solution_html: Squares, rectangles, parallelograms are all quadrilaterals because all of them have four line segments and all are closed figures.

questions.question_html: Give reason for square is also a parallelogram.
questions.solution_html: In a parallelogram opposite sides are equal and parallel and in a square opposite side are equal and all the sides have same length. So, yes a square is a special parallelogram.

questions.question_html: Give reason for a square can be thought of as a special rhombus.
questions.solution_html: All side of a rhombus are equal and a square also has all of its sides equals to each other with all the interior angles of 90o. A rhombus with each angle a right angle becomes a square. So, a square can be thought of as a special rhombus.

questions.question_html: A figure is said to be regular, if its sides are equal in length and angles are equal in measure. Can you identify the regular quadrilateral?
questions.solution_html: A square is a 'regular' quadrilateral.

questions.question_html: Examine whether the given figure is polygon and if not why?
questions.solution_html: No, it is not a polygon because it is not a closed figure.

questions.question_html: Examine whether the given figure is polygon and if not why?
questions.solution_html: Yes, it is a polygon as it is made of 6 line segments and is closed.

questions.solution_html: No, it is not a polygon. It’s a circle and is not made of line segments.

questions.question_html: Examine whether the given figure is polygon and if not why?
questions.solution_html: No, it’s not a polygon as it is not only made of line segments but has a circular part as well.

questions.solution_html: As the given figure is made of 4 line segments, therefore it is a quadrilateral.

questions.solution_html: The given figure is a pentagon because it is made up of 5 line segments.

questions.solution_html: The given figure is of octagon because it is made of 8 line segments.

questions.question_html: Draw a rough sketch of a regular hexagon. Connecting any three of its vertices, draw a triangle. Identify the type of the triangle you have drawn.
questions.solution_html: The triangle drawn is an obtuse-angled and isosceles triangle.

questions.question_html: Draw a rough sketch of a regular octagon. (Use squared paper if you wish). Draw a rectangle by joining exactly four of the vertices of the octagon.
A diagonal is a line segment that joins any two vertices of the polygon and is not a side of the polygon. Draw a rough sketch of a pentagon and draw its diagonals.
